entry.description: Agent-to-agent task envelopes and capability card discovery.
Six-dimension classification axes for agent capability and autonomy.
CoALA decision loop: perceive, retrieve memory, reason, select action, execute, update memory.
Goal-directed behavior with hierarchical task decomposition in language agents.
Container grouping command area, storage area, and attached source planes.
Canonical workflow patterns for production agent systems.
Three-layer enterprise ontology informing agent role and goal hierarchy.
Label-based propagation rules for context and tool result boundaries.
Harness tuple H=(E,T,C,S,L,V) maps to runtime envelope components.
(α, C, τ) parameters for memory retention, capacity, and temporal decay.
Standard tool discovery, resource access, and prompt template protocol.
Message history, system prompt, few-shot, and user/agent message typing.
Graph-structured reasoning topology for multi-hop deliberation.
Meta-graph defines primitives used to describe all other subgraphs.
Orchestrator-workers, prompt chains, routing, parallelization, and evaluator-optimizer workflow topology.
Think-act separation with stage-1 blocking bias and stage-2 deep deliberation.
SMT-synthesized minimal permission sets for tool invocation.
InfoPlane progressive disclosure pattern linking light index to on-demand context materialization.
Sparse-dense-graph retrieval fusion pipeline from query through rank fusion and reranker to context.
Two-stage permission model, sandbox execution, proxy egress, and injection scanning on tool results.

entry.source_name: Xi et al. LLM Agent Survey
entry.description: Brain (LLM core), perception (context input), action (tool use) tripartite split.
